chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD. C.P. No.D-3328 of 2018. DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE For orders on office objection. For hearing of MA-14966/18. For hearing of main case. 27.03.2019. M/s. Willayat Ali Khan and Aijaz Ali Magsi, Advocate a/w petitioners. Mr. Muhammad Ismail Bhutto, Addl: A.G alongwith SIP Abdul Hafeez Soomro, incharge PP Bello PS Sijawal. Mrs. Sobia Bhatti, A.P.G. for the State. === Mr. Bhutto learned Addl. A.G. submits that I.O. has recorded the statmenet of the petitioner No.1 on 16.1.2019 and copy of the same is available in the Court file, he has nothing to file on the same. We have gone through the police papers and statement of the petitioner No.1 recorded by I.O, which reveals that the petitioner No.1 has categorically stated that she has never been abducted or compelled to marry with the petitioner No.2, hence criminal case/F.I.R lodged by her father/respondent No.5 is false, fabricated has no truth. She,therefore, stated that case may be disposed off and no coercive action may be taken against them. Confronting with the above, counsel for the petitioners present states that he will be satisfied if directions be issued to the I.O. for disposal of the case in view of the statement of petitioner No.1, recorded by him and will not press this petition. Learned Addl. A.G and learned A.P.G. have no objection; however, they submit that the I.O. shall dispose of the case on the basis of statmenet of the petitioner No.1(alleged abductee) and file such report under section 173, Cr.P.C. before the Court of competent jurisdiction, who shall decide it in accordance with law.

Learned Addl. A.G and learned A.P.G. have no objection; however, they submit that the I.O. shall dispose of the case on the basis of statmenet of the petitioner No.1(alleged abductee) and file such report under section 173, Cr.P.C. before the Court of competent jurisdiction, who shall decide it in accordance with law. As far as withdrawal of the petition is concerned, both, learned Addl. A.G. and A.P.G have no objection. In view of above development, this petition seems to have achieved its purpose, therefore, is hereby disposed off alongwith all pending application(s). JUDGE. JUDGE. g
